summary: AI replenishment platform for fresh-grocery forecasting, ordering, and waste reduction.
editor_note: Fresh departments are not normal inventory problems: shelf life, scan errors, spoilage, promotions, and weather distort demand every day. Freshflow models 120+ signals and gives buyers exception-based order proposals instead of another BI screen. The useful proof is operational: customers cite 93% proposal acceptance, up to 30% less waste, and up to 4% more sales. The risk is whether retailer data quality can keep the model honest.

product_evidence.claim: The official site breaks the product into Forecast, Inventory, Optimization, and Freshflow App modules for fresh-food replenishment decisions.
Freshflow publishes customer-facing outcome claims including 120+ demand signals, 93% order-proposal acceptance, up to 30% less waste, and up to 4% more revenue.

market_context.primary_user: European grocery retailers and fresh-department buyers balancing availability, waste, and daily order proposals.
market_context.differentiation: Freshflow optimizes fresh ordering around shelf life and store-level demand instead of treating produce like durable inventory.
market_context.why_now: The June 2026 Series A gives the company capital to expand across a European grocery market under waste and margin pressure.

risks.claim: The open question is whether each retailer's POS, spoilage, promotion, and scan-error data is clean enough for reliable store-level automation.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
